features.title: One Ghost, real decisions
features.body: Each commando is a valuable field asset. Support units can extend an operation, but the loss of the Ghost remains the critical failure point.

features.title: Hunt neural critters
features.body: Some jungle specimens flee when approached. Stun them first, then harvest before they recover and vanish into cover.

features.title: Builds stay personal
features.body: Commandos may specialize as marksmen, assault troops, demolitions experts, or field support.

features.title: Craft tactical constructs
features.body: Lights, walls, stations, turrets, companion tools, and Maelstrom Mines provide temporary battlefield control.

evolutions.name: Black Forager
evolutions.change: Low HP scout-worker. Mostly tests player aim and reveals where the swarm is spreading.

evolutions.name: Brown Gnawer
evolutions.change: Gains more HP and a faster bite, making ignored worker packs dangerous.

evolutions.name: Orange Stripper
evolutions.change: Gains bonus damage against structures and deconstruct targets, punishing static defenses.

evolutions.name: Red Ravager
evolutions.change: Fast raider with high structure damage and enough HP to slip through weak perimeter fire.

evolutions.name: Black Brood
evolutions.change: Very fragile melee flood unit that appears in numbers.

evolutions.name: Brown Brood
evolutions.change: More movement speed and HP, better at surrounding isolated Ghosts.

evolutions.name: Orange Brood
evolutions.change: Much faster attack cooldown; dangerous if allowed to stack on a player.

evolutions.name: Red Brood
evolutions.change: Tiny berserker variant with high speed and burst damage, forcing quick repositioning.

evolutions.name: Black Skitter
evolutions.change: Basic fast melee enemy: low HP, low damage, short vision.

evolutions.name: Brown Skitter
evolutions.change: Gains +HP and movement speed, becoming reliable chase pressure.

evolutions.name: Orange Ripper
evolutions.change: Switches to a sharper claw profile: higher damage and shorter cooldown.

evolutions.name: Red Ripper
evolutions.change: Elite hunter with max speed, high damage, and enough durability to punish wounded players.

evolutions.name: Black Spitter
evolutions.change: Short-range spine attack with modest damage.

evolutions.name: Brown Spitter
evolutions.change: Gains HP and +1 weapon range, letting it fire from behind melee mobs.

evolutions.name: Orange Needler
evolutions.change: Swaps to a faster needle weapon with better cooldown and higher damage.

evolutions.name: Red Needler
evolutions.change: Long-range elite shooter with high damage, forcing players to break line of sight or snipe it first.

evolutions.name: Black Burrower
evolutions.change: Slow setup ambusher with low damage spikes.

evolutions.name: Brown Burrower
evolutions.change: More HP and stronger spine damage, holding narrow paths better.

evolutions.name: Orange Impaler
evolutions.change: Longer spine range and higher splash pressure, threatening grouped players.

evolutions.name: Red Impaler
evolutions.change: Elite area-control unit with very long reach and lethal line damage if not cleared quickly.

evolutions.name: Black Matron
evolutions.change: Light support threat with low HP and short-range harassment.

evolutions.name: Brown Matron
evolutions.change: Gains HP and faster movement so it can stay behind the front line.

evolutions.name: Orange Hexer
evolutions.change: Switches to a longer-range disruption weapon/effect to pressure priority targets.

evolutions.name: Red Hexer
evolutions.change: High-priority support unit with long range and punishing debuff-style pressure.

evolutions.name: Black Wing
evolutions.change: Light air threat with low HP and short-range poke.

evolutions.name: Brown Wing
evolutions.change: Gains movement speed and HP, making it harder to kite.

evolutions.name: Orange Razorwing
evolutions.change: Upgraded bouncing glaive damage for harassment against grouped players.

evolutions.name: Red Razorwing
evolutions.change: Fast elite flyer with higher damage and longer range, demanding anti-air weapon swaps.

evolutions.name: Black Siegewing
evolutions.change: Slow air artillery with low damage but long enough range to teach target priority.

evolutions.name: Brown Siegewing
evolutions.change: Gains HP and stronger siege damage against structures.

evolutions.name: Orange Shellwing
evolutions.change: Longer range and higher impact damage, forcing the squad to move or counter-snipe.

evolutions.name: Red Shellwing
evolutions.change: Elite siege threat with very long range and heavy structure/player pressure.

evolutions.name: Black Bloater
evolutions.change: Slow fragile special unit with a small death warning.

evolutions.name: Brown Bloater
evolutions.change: Gains HP and a larger death burst radius.

evolutions.name: Orange Plaguebearer
evolutions.change: Death effect becomes plague-style damage over an area, punishing close-range kills.

evolutions.name: Red Plaguebearer
evolutions.change: Large plague/explosion death effect that can break grouped players if killed carelessly.

evolutions.name: Black Crusher
evolutions.change: Slow tank with high HP but low early damage.

evolutions.name: Brown Crusher
evolutions.change: Gains armor and a larger HP pool, requiring sustained fire.

evolutions.name: Orange Mauler
evolutions.change: Gains speed and a stronger melee weapon, becoming a real chase threat.

evolutions.name: Red Mauler
evolutions.change: Boss-like elite with heavy armor, huge HP, and punishing melee damage.

upgrades.title: Weapon unlocks
upgrades.body: Level 1 in a path authorizes construction of that weapon package. A package includes both the Ghost's basic fire profile and a dedicated target ability.

upgrades.title: Weapon switching
upgrades.body: Only one primary package may be equipped at a time. Previously crafted packages remain available, letting a commando swap from rifle control to splash, slow, stun, or precision work as the mission changes.

upgrades.title: Rifle path
upgrades.body: The rifle is the balanced field package: dependable auto-fire, solid range, and a practical target shot for priority kills. It should be the baseline other weapons trade against.

upgrades.title: Shotgun path
upgrades.body: The shotgun package favors close contact. Its basic fire can be risky or short-ranged, but its target ability can deliver stun, knockback, or cone disruption when the swarm closes.

upgrades.title: Sniper path
upgrades.body: The sniper package trades rate of fire for reach and decisive target pressure. A weaker or slower auto-attack can justify a stronger single-target strike against elites and high-value threats.

upgrades.title: Flamer or acid path
upgrades.body: The flame/acid package controls ground rather than winning direct duels. Its target ability can slow, corrode, confuse, or leave lingering zones that punish packed enemies.

upgrades.title: Explosive path
upgrades.body: The explosive package is anti-pack ordnance. Basic fire may be awkward or delayed, while the target ability delivers splash, burst timing, or area denial against clustered Zerg.

upgrades.title: Survival: max health
upgrades.body: Raises Ghost vitality so a commando can survive surrounds, night collapses, and failed withdrawals long enough to recover or be rescued.

upgrades.title: Survival: armor and shields
upgrades.body: Adds defensive layers such as armor plating, shield capacity, or regeneration systems. These upgrades reduce sudden deaths without replacing good positioning.

upgrades.title: Mobility and vision
upgrades.body: Improves patrol speed, scouting range, kiting, and withdrawal options. Mobility upgrades help the squad choose fights instead of being pinned by them.

upgrades.title: Harvest efficiency
upgrades.body: Improves field gathering so the Ghost spends less time exposed at trees, rocks, crystals, organic growth, and other resource points.

upgrades.title: Construct utility
upgrades.body: Improves temporary structures: better repair value, deconstruction refunds, alerts, deployable support, and the ability to hold a pocket slightly longer before abandoning it.

upgrades.title: Maelstrom Mines
upgrades.body: Burrowed stun devices used to interrupt rushes, cover retreats, or set up neural critter harvests. Upgrades may alter radius, stun duration, arming time, or elite interrupt value.

upgrades.title: Companion unlocks
upgrades.body: Authorizes robotic support, controlled Zerg specimens, or resurrected organisms. Companions add tactical options, but they are support assets rather than the main army.

upgrades.title: Companion upgrades
upgrades.body: Improves companion count, durability, or battlefield role. Companion power still remains tied to Ghost survival, so losing the commando costs the squad.

gameplayLoop.title: Five-night operation
gameplayLoop.body: Hold the sector through five night cycles. Extraction conditions and final resistance remain unconfirmed.

gameplayLoop.title: One commander per player
gameplayLoop.body: Each player fields one Ghost. Support units extend options, but the commando remains the operation's center.

gameplayLoop.title: Daylight creates choices
gameplayLoop.body: Use safe hours to decide what matters most: supplies, weapon training, fortification, or relocation.

gameplayLoop.title: Night punishes delay
gameplayLoop.body: Once enemy movement increases, positions must either hold quickly or be abandoned before losses compound.

gameplayLoop.title: Equipment defines role
gameplayLoop.body: A crafted weapon package changes both routine fire and the Ghost's main target ability.

gameplayLoop.title: Failure costs momentum
gameplayLoop.body: Death removes field assets and carried resources, but retained training lets the run continue.

resources.title: Trees
resources.body: Common mineral-heavy supply nodes with minor gas yield.

resources.title: Bushes
resources.body: Fast organic harvest points suited to moving patrols.

resources.title: Rocks
resources.body: Mineral-rich deposits requiring a safer work window.

resources.title: Crystals
resources.body: Vespene-rich formations usually found deeper in hostile territory.

resources.title: Organic growth
resources.body: Balanced biological matter, often near contested ground.

resources.title: Neural critters
resources.body: Mobile wildlife specimens. Stun or trap them before harvest.

resources.title: Harvesting tradeoff
resources.body: A harvesting Ghost cannot provide supporting fire until the task is broken off.

notes.title: Reuse unused abilities as spells
notes.body: Review dormant StarCraft abilities and retain only effects that read clearly in combat.

notes.title: Command card stays readable
notes.body: Keep the command card operationally clear. Weapon packages should swap the main target ability instead of adding extra buttons.

notes.title: Few active hotbar abilities
notes.body: Limit universal field commands to Heal, Speed, and Stun; weapon-specific target abilities carry tactical variety.

notes.title: Psi Emitter feasibility
notes.body: Test a deployable emitter that lures hostile movement or amplifies nearby field effects.
